summary: Tested exercise_preferences field wiring into workout generation (previously dead code). All 14 tests passed (100%). Verified: (1) build_blueprint() includes exercise_preferences in returned dict, (2) no regression for workouts without preferences, (3) 'hate burpees' advisory is honored — Claude chose Jump Rope/Mountain Climbers EMOM instead of Burpee Intervals across 3-day bodyweight loss_fat workout, (4) 'love Romanian Deadlifts, hate burpees' honored — Romanian Deadlift appeared in 4-day full_gym build_muscle workout with no burpees. Tests confirmed field flows from request → blueprint dict → LLM prompt → generated exercise names.
critical_code_review_comments: Advisory prompt guidance works well. Since Claude picks from the provided Options list, the disliked exercise avoidance only activates when the disliked exercise actually appears in the slot's options list. This is by design (as noted in the spec) and working correctly.
context_for_next_testing_agent: exercise_preferences feature is fully wired and tested. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_exercise_preferences.py covers unit (blueprint structure), regression (no prefs), hate-burpees (bodyweight+lose_fat → conditioning slot has burpees as option, Claude avoids it), love-RDL+hate-burpees (full_gym+build_muscle → hip_hinge slot has RDL as option, Claude picks it). Test user cbd82a69-3a37-48c2-88e8-0fe95081fa4b has admin email so bypasses quota check.
rca_of_issue: Feature was wired correctly in this iteration: build_blueprint() at line 2826 returns exercise_preferences via getattr(req, 'exercise_preferences', None). Prompt construction at lines 3286-3292 injects advisory guidance. Claude correctly uses advisory guidance to pick preferred/avoid disliked exercises from the options list.
